citation: Porter, M., Strang, R., & Costello, D. (2024). Round Table: Grants & Gaming.
summary:
# Summary

The provided text is not an empirical research article but rather a structured outline and resource compilation for a professional development session titled "Round Table: Grants & Gaming," presented by Molly Porter, Rebecca Strang, and Danielle Costello in 2024. The document serves as a practical guide for librarians seeking to integrate gaming into library services through funded programming. It does not contain original data collection, hypothesis testing, or statistical analysis; therefore, standard empirical metrics such as sample size, methodology, and quantitative findings are not applicable.

The primary objective of the text is to disseminate information regarding funding opportunities and best practices for grant writing specifically tailored to gaming initiatives in libraries. The presenters highlight specific examples of successful applications, notably referencing "Molly’s Zombie Game Files for ALA’s Thinking Money for Kids Grant." This example illustrates a concrete application of games-based library services (GBLS), where a game mechanic (a zombie game) is utilized to teach financial literacy ("Thinking Money for Kids"). The text provides contact information for further inquiry regarding this specific project, indicating its role as a case study or exemplar within the broader discussion.

A significant portion of the document is dedicated to listing external resources that support grant acquisition and gaming community management. Under "Grants to Explore," it identifies specific funding bodies and programs, including the Games & Gaming Round Table’s "Game On! Grant" and the American Library Association’s (ALA) "Libraries Transforming Communities Grant." It also references general grant writing literature, such as works by Stephanie Gerding and Pamela H. MacKellar, and digital resources like Webjunction webinars and university Libguides (UConn, Adelphi University). These resources are presented as tools to help librarians navigate the complexities of securing funding for technology and programming initiatives.

Furthermore, the text addresses the operational aspect of maintaining online gaming communities by outlining the "GameRT Twitch Policy." This section provides explicit guidelines for behavior on the Games & Gaming Round Table’s Twitch channel, emphasizing respect, zero tolerance for hate speech, and procedures for handling harassment. This highlights a critical component of GBLS: the need for robust community management strategies to ensure safe and inclusive digital environments for patrons engaging in gaming activities. The policy includes directives for moderators and encourages private communication for sensitive issues, reflecting professional standards for online community moderation.

The document concludes with contact information and links to further resources, including the GameRT blog, Facebook pages, and public resource repositories. It directs questions to a dedicated email address (gamert@ala.org), reinforcing its function as an informational hub rather than a research report. In the context of a literature review on Games-Based Library Services, this text contributes by documenting the practical infrastructure supporting GBLS: available funding streams, educational materials for grant writing, and community management protocols. It underscores that successful implementation of gaming services often requires external funding and careful moderation, areas where professional organizations like ALA provide structured support. The absence of empirical data limits its utility for statistical synthesis but positions it as a valuable resource for understanding the administrative and logistical frameworks underpinning library gaming programs.
sourceText:
Round Table Round Table: Grants & Gaming
w/ Molly Porter, Rebecca Strang, Danielle Costello
Files For Today’s Talk ● Molly’s Zombie Game Files for ALA’s Thinking Money for Kids Grant ● Contact Molly at: mporter@wfplibrary.org
Grants to Explore ● Games & Gaming Round Table, Game On! Grant ● ALA, Libraries Transforming Communities Grant ● Library Grants by Stephanie Gerding
Grant Writing Resources to Help You Out
Lists & Articles ● Librarian and Research Knowledge Space Grants Resource List ● School Library Journal 5 Grant Writing Tips ● Webjunction Elements of a Good Grant Proposal
Webinars & Courses ● Webjunction, Grantseeking for Libraries: Strategies and Tips ● Webjunction, Winning Library Grants: Shortcuts and Strategies for Success ● Programing Librarians, Grant Writing 101: Basics for Programming Librarians ● ALA Public Programs Office Writing Grant Applications
Libguides ● Uconn Grant Support for Librarians ● Adelphi University Grant Resources & Grant Writing
Books ● Winning Grants by Pamela H. MacKellar; Stephanie K. Gerding ● Writing Successful Technology Grant Proposals by Pamela H. MacKellar
GameRT Twitch Policy 1. Be kind and respectful to one another 2. Hate speech of any type will not be tolerated 3. We reserve the right to remove anyone from the chat for any reason 4. If you are feeling harassed by anyone, please notify a GameRT twitch mod and one of us will assist you 5. If you'd rather talk privately about anything, please send us a direct message
Learn More About GameRT Blog: bit.ly/GameRTBlog Facebook: bit.ly/GameRTLeague & bit.ly/GameRTFacebook Resources: bit.ly/GameRTPublicResources Website: bit.ly/GameRTWebsite Questions? Email gamert@ala.org
